summary: Matched the fighter camera module by replacing asm/melee/ft/ftcamera.s with src/melee/ft/ftcamera.c and wiring ftcamera.c.o into obj_files.mk. The C version implements the former 0x80076018-0x80076320 camera-box routines, adds a small six-float camera data struct, refines FighterData and CameraBox layouts in fighter.h, updates ftlib camera access to use the newly identified CameraBox field x48.z, and documents that Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset corresponds to stGetPlyDeadUp based on an assert string. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/ftcamera.c with C implementations of func_80076018, func_80076064, func_800761C8, func_800762F4, and func_80076320.
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftcamera.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/ftcamera.s.o.
Introduced struct UnkFloat6_Camera as two Vec3 values and used it to model the six camera-related floats read from ftData->x3C.
Adjusted ftData layout in fighter.h so filler_x14 ends before a new x3C pointer to struct UnkFloat6_Camera, followed by filler_x40.
Refined CameraBox layout in fighter.h: split the previous filler into named or typed fields including x8, x28, x2C Vec2, x34 Vec3, x40 Vec2, and x48 Vec3.
Updated func_80086B80 in ftlib.c from returning cameraBox->x50 to returning cameraBox->x48.z after the CameraBox layout refinement.
Added a stage.c comment noting that Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset is named stGetPlyDeadUp according to an assert in ftcamera.c.
smart_moves: Used the assert string from the original ftcamera assembly data section, "stGetPlyDeadUp() - center_pos.y != 0.0F", as evidence for documenting Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset naming.
Modeled the contiguous six-float camera parameters as two Vec3 fields, which made the repeated scaling and copy operations readable while preserving the observed memory layout.
Updated shared headers before consuming the newly identified offsets, allowing ftcamera.c and ftlib.c to use the same CameraBox interpretation.
Preserved known but still-uncertain names by keeping placeholder symbol names such as func_80076018 and UnkFloat6_Camera where stronger evidence was not shown.

Matched awkward compiler behavior with no-op expression statements such as spC.xC; and ft->x2C_facing_direction + 1.0f; as noted by inline comments, indicating a pragmatic matching tactic rather than over-cleaning the source.
decomp_lessons: Camera-related fighter data at ftData offset x3C can be represented as a pointer to a six-float structure, here UnkFloat6_Camera with two Vec3 members.
CameraBox offset work should be propagated to all users: changing x50 into x48.z required updating ftlib.c accessors as well as the CameraBox struct definition.
Assert strings embedded in deleted assembly can provide useful cross-system naming clues; in this PR, an ftcamera assert informed a stage function comment.
When a function copies raw float/vector regions into multiple CameraBox fields, typed Vec2 and Vec3 fields can expose intent and reduce filler without necessarily fully naming every semantic field.
For early matches, keeping unknown function names and adding explanatory comments may be safer than inventing precise API names without evidence.
naming_conventions: Retained unknown function names in the func_8007xxxx style for unmatched or not-yet-renamed routines: func_80076018, func_80076064, func_800761C8, func_800762F4, func_80076320.
Used offset-style struct field names for partially understood layouts, such as x3C, x8, x28, x2C, x34, x40, and x48.

Used descriptive comments to record likely names without changing symbols, for example Camera_CopyPlayerPositionToCameraBoxPosition, Fighter_UpdateCameraBox, Camera_UpdatePlayerCameraBoxPosition, and Fighter_GetCameraBonePos.
Named the temporary six-float struct UnkFloat6_Camera, explicitly marking uncertainty while capturing field shape.
Preserved existing project style for fighter pointers and objects, including Fighter* ft, HSD_GObj* gobj, and gobj->user_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced an assembly object with a C object only after matching the ftcamera text range well enough to remove the asm file from obj_files.mk.
Used small helper func_80076018 to reproduce the assembly pattern that scales six consecutive floats by a multiplier.
Matched facing-direction branches against 1.0f to decide whether camera_box x40 values are assigned directly or negated and whether x28 is 1.0f or -1.0f.
Kept seemingly useless expressions, including spC.xC; and ft->x2C_facing_direction + 1.0f;, because they affected code generation according to inline comments.
Preserved the original assert condition and file/string evidence in func_80076320, including __assert("ftcamera.c", 137, "stGetPlyDeadUp() - center_pos.y != 0.0F").
Used direct struct assignment for vector copies, such as camera_box->x2C = camera_box->x40 and camera_box->x34 = camera_box->x48, reflecting the assembly's copy behavior through typed fields.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; inferred lessons are based only on title, changed files, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_80076064, func_800761C8, func_800762F4, or func_80076320.
Find later changes to CameraBox field names x28, x2C, x34, x40, and x48.
Find later changes to UnkFloat6_Camera or ftData->x3C to determine the real semantic name of the six camera floats.
Search for additional assert strings referencing stGetPlyDeadUp or ftcamera.c to validate the Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset naming note.
Find later cleanup of matching no-op expressions such as spC.xC; or ft->x2C_facing_direction + 1.0f.
